subject: UX & Research
title: Tone of Voice in UX Writing
description: A micro-lesson that makes Tone of Voice in UX Writing usable.

hook.text: Mailchimp's voice is 'fun but not silly, confident but not cocky.' Every button and message follows that tone.

buildup.text: UX writing isn't just clear labels. It's a consistent personality that builds trust and shapes how users feel.

discovery.text: Mailchimp published their Content Style Guide publicly. It became the template for UX writing guides everywhere.

twist.text: Humor in UX writing can backfire. A joke on an error page is charming once but irritating on the tenth failure.

climax.text: Define your product's voice in 3-4 principles. Apply them consistently. Users feel the personality even in microcopy.

punchline.text: Voice builds personality. Consistent tone builds trust across every interaction.

quiz.question: Why did Mailchimp's Content Style Guide become influential?
options: It showed how consistent voice and tone create product personality across all UX writing
It was the longest style guide ever published
It introduced a new programming language for content
